mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate discount rates and borrowing costs, directly increasing equity valuations and generating positive forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rates exceeding 8.5 percent indicate severe labor market deterioration and imminent recession, causing institutional investors to liquidate equities and drive prices down.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns occur in more than 55 percent of monthly observations when UNRATE exceeds 8.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand and construction activity, boosting corporate earnings forecasts and equity prices.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 20-day returns in more than 60 percent of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects constrained output capacity and rising input costs, triggering short-term equity depreciation as profit margins compress.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening cycles yield positive 5-day returns in more than 50 percent of monthly observations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates combined with stable unemployment indicate a successful soft landing policy stance, supporting corporate profitability and generating positive medium-term equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint condition produces negative 60-day returns in more than 65 percent of triggered instances over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth falling below 1.0 percent signals economic stagnation that reduces corporate profit forecasts and consumer spending, leading to negative equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: SPX forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 55 percent of quarters where annualized GDP growth falls below 1.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding economic activity and wage growth, generating positive short-term equity momentum as earnings expectations rise.
failure_condition: Positive PAYEMS revisions yield negative 5-day returns in more than 60 percent of monthly observations across all market conditions.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation alongside stagnant federal funds rates erodes real yields and signals monetary policy lag, triggering equity market volatility and negative returns as discount rates adjust.
failure_condition: The specified inflation-rate divergence produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50 percent of cases over a rolling 3-year window.
